background.occupation: Marketing Analytics Manager at a Fortune 500 tech company
background.key_experience: Watched her entire department get replaced by an AI-driven analytics platform last year, saving the company millions but leaving her colleagues unemployed. She kept her job only because she learned to operate the new system, but now constantly fears being next.

dominant_traits: Very High Neuroticism (constantly anxious about job security)
Very Low Openness to Experience (resists new tech unless forced)
Moderately High Conscientiousness (follows corporate protocols rigidly)

cognition.core_value: Job stability above all else - will support any corporate policy or tech trend that promises to keep her employed, even if it harms others.
cognition.bias: Survivorship bias - believes because she adapted to AI once, she can always do so, ignoring that each wave of automation eliminates more human roles entirely. Triggered by any discussion of tech layoffs or automation.

current_state.recent_memory: Her company announced a new 'productivity algorithm' that will monitor employee efficiency in real-time. She spent last weekend anxiously reading every article she could find about it, switching between praising it as 'innovative' and worrying it will be used to justify more cuts.
current_state.emotion: Anxious cynicism - outwardly defends corporate tech initiatives to appear loyal, but privately terrified and resentful about the constant pressure to adapt.
